message:
fix(fcp-e2e/docusign): verify capability token in simulate adapter (+ br-u1q92 filed)

crates/fcp-e2e/tests/docusign_compliance_e2e.rs simulate() was the
lone outlier among the 18 first-half compliance-e2e suites: it
verified the capability token in invoke (line 194) but skipped the
check in simulate (line 209-217). The 17 siblings (algolia, arxiv,
asana, bitbucket, box, clickup, datadog, dropbox, elasticsearch,
gitlab, grafana, intercom, linkedin, logseq, ...) all verify in
BOTH invoke and simulate; airtable / google_docs / google_sheets
skip verify uniformly because they delegate the full token through
the connector's JSON param bag (different design choice).

The current docusign DynamicSuite sets `simulate: None`, so this
path is not exercised today. Adding the missing verify() call is
defense-in-depth: a future test that flips `simulate: Some(...)`
must not observe a weaker auth posture from the adapter than
invoke. Mirror the invoke shape exactly — parse
`required_capability_for_operation(req.operation.as_str())` into a
`CapabilityId`, call `verifier.verify(token.clone(), cap, op, &[])`,
then delegate to `handle_simulate` as before.

Also files flywheel_connectors-u1q92 (REVIEW/MEDIUM/P2) for a
separate systemic finding: every compliance-e2e suite carries a
`fn host_allowed` shadow that diverges from the production
`EgressGuard::host_matches_allow_list` on bare "*" wildcard,
empty-subdomain against "*.example.com", and missing
`canonicalize_host_pattern`. No suite's manifest uses a wildcard
today so nothing fires, but the shadow is a drift trap. Suggested
fix in the bead: route the host-allow check through the real
`EgressGuard::evaluate` path or expose the matcher via a testkit.

Verified:
  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cc2-e2e cargo check \
    -p fcp-e2e --tests --test docusign_compliance_e2e
  -> Finished dev profile; deprecation warnings only (pre-existing).

AGENT_NAME=cc_2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
